aka.commentary: El creador discontinuó esta palabra. Fue reemplazado por "natu".
aka.definition: equis, cruz, interseccion; superposición, solapamiento [forma alternativa de la palabra natu]
aka.etymology: 过 (guò): mandarín, cruzar, atravesar; across: inglés, across

ako.commentary: Prácticamente desconocido fuera de la comunidad coreana de toki pona.
ako.definition: (interjección genérica cuyo significado depende del contexto)

alente.commentary: Creado de broma como el "nimisin final" - cualquier palabra nueva después ya está incluido semánticamente en "alente", entonces puede ser rechazado por ser "alente pero más específico".
alente.definition: el grupo conformado por la suma de todos los conceptos imaginables, excepto los cuales ya están conceptualizados en la lista establecida de palabras en Toki Pona ordenadas según su frecuencia relativa de uso
alente.etymology: ale ante: toki pona, everything else

alu.definition: (partícula) [usada después de la oración principal, introduciendo una frase contextual]
alu.etymology: la: toki pona, (indicate context for a following sentence)

an.etymology: 函 (hán): Chinese, attributed location/thing for something, slot. aisle, receptacle, home. passage (of text). to 'slot in'/'fit in'/be(come) accustomed

antikontitutonelema.commentary: Varias -ti- sílabas. Si se desea, se puede cambiar una (especialmente la primera) o todas las "ti" sílabas para una fonología más clásica de toki pona.

apelo.commentary: Las bebidas no necesitan hacer alcohólicas, también incluye a los snacks.
apelo.definition: relajando con amigos con una bebida/cóctel (o dos)
apelo.etymology: apéro: francés, apéritif (bebida alcohólica servida antes de una comida como aperitivo)

apoko.definition: el 💀 emoji; una adaptación toki pona del meme "I forgor 💀" (es como decir "olviré 💀" y no "olvidé")

awase.commentary: jan Lenoka también elaboró la semántica para implicar "chocar con, hacer contacto con".

eki.commentary: Se recomienda usar "natu" en su lugar.
eki.definition: equis, cruz, interseccion; superposición, solapamiento [forma alternativa de la palabra natu]

eliki.commentary: an experience with the quality of being deeply felt or important to oneself while defying classification as positive, negative, or a simply described admixture of positive and negative

enko.commentary:
Considered deprecated by the author: "mi pali e nimi enko a a
open la pilin mi li sama
nimi enko li pona, jan mute o kepeken
taso kepeken pi toki pona la mi kama sona
nimi ante li ken toki e wile toki pi nimi enko
la ona li suli ala"
enko.definition: (equivalente espacial de tenpo) espacio, lugar, distancia, tamaño (largo, ancho, alto, profundidad, área, volumen)
enko.etymology: tenpo: toki pona, time, period, situation

epikule.commentary: También conocido como kulepiku. Básicamente solo un glifo compuesto que fue listado como una palabra.

ete.definition: fuera de, aparte de; más allá de, más que
ete.etymology: öte: turco, beyond, further, over; ettë: quenya, outside

ewe.definition: piedra, grava, roca, guijarro; lava, magma
ewe.etymology: 岩 (iwa): japonés, rock, boulder; kiwen: toki pona, rock, stone, hard thing

i.commentary: "i" was proposed as a replacement for "iki" in a poll in 2002. However, "i" failed to get enough votes. "iki" was instead replaced by "ona".
i.definition: él, ella, ello, lo, la, le, ellos, ellas [forma alternativa de la palabra ona]

iki.commentary: En una encuesta en 2002, "iki" fue considerado demasiado similar a "ike" y fue reemplazado por "ona".
iki.definition: él, ella, ello, lo, la, le, ellos, ellas [forma alternativa de la palabra ona]
iki.etymology: იგი (igi): georgiano, (third-person singular masculine, feminine, and gender neutral pronoun)

inta.commentary: A particle that allows the speaker to specify the level of information they have about a situation. As a modifier, it means "at least" or "definitely", indicating knowledge of one thing but not necessarily other things.
inta.definition: por lo menos, definitivamente; todavía, aún

ipi.commentary: "ipi" was proposed as a replacement for "iki" in a poll in 2002. However, "ipi" failed to get enough votes. "iki" was instead replaced by "ona".
ipi.definition: él, ella, ello, lo, la, le, ellos, ellas [forma alternativa de la palabra ona]

iseki.commentary: Intended as an alt for namako that removes the "additional, extra" definition. used to emphasize that these details are core to something, and not superfluous.
iseki.definition: flor; adorno, accesorio, especia; ornamental, estético, bello, hermoso, floreciente

itomi.commentary: Coined in reference to the song ‘Mia Khalifa’ by iLOVEFRiDay, where "hit or miss" was a prominent lyric. Sources disagree on who coined the word.
itomi.definition: hablar mal (indirectamente), no respetar; alegría por el sufrimiento de otros
itomi.etymology: hit or miss: inglés, hit or miss

iwa.definition: malodorous chemical substance, such as sulfur, hydrogen sulfide, chlorine gas, bromine, white phosphorus, or selenide compounds

ja.commentary: Can also be combined with lo to mark modified or compound prepositions, marking the end of a preposition marked by lo and the start of the prepositional object.
ja.definition: (particle) [marks the previous word(s) as a single preverb]

jalan.commentary: In a poll in 2002, "jalan" was proposed as a replacement for "noka". However, "jalan" failed to get enough votes.
jalan.definition: pie, pata [forma alternativa de la palabra noka]
jalan.etymology: jalan: finés, afoot; jalka: finés, foot

jans.commentary: Not coined directly, but developed out of repeated use in the early ma pona pi toki pona Discord server community. jans was repeatedly used because of the humor in adding an English plural suffix to a Toki Pona word.
jans.definition: un grupo específico de los primeros miembros del servidor de Discord ma pona pi toki pona
jans.etymology: jan: toki pona, person, people; -/z/: inglés, (plural suffix)

jepi.commentary: Coined indepentently multiple times over several years, non-wuwojiti variant of the word jipi.

jipi.commentary: Coined indepentently multiple times over several years.

jonke.commentary: Creado en referencia al fandom de Untitled Goose Game.

josuta.commentary: josuta is not the same as ko. or kiwen. or telo. It is its own thing. josuta covers the concepts of elasticity, the quality of things that are rubbery, and stretchy; elasticity. This is not to be confused with ko. josuta is a seperate thing, that is, in any way, not covered by "ko", "telo", "kiwen", or "suwi".

ju.definition: (palabra reservada por Sonja Lang para su uso en el futuro)

jule.definition: indeterminado, inconcluso, tentativo; impredecible, inestable, volátil; vacilar, temblar, tambalearse, zozobrar, tiritar
jule.etymology: 揺れる (yureru): japonés, shake, vibrate, oscillate, waver

jume.commentary: Experimental, para ver cómo el significado podría ser aumentado, pero realmente nunca fue usado. Ningún grupo de significados se resolvió antes que el experimento se terminó, pero un significado potencial que al creador particularmente le gustaba era "fantasía" o "irrealidad", sin connotación negativa.

jupi.commentary: Coined indepentently multiple times over several years. Non-wuwojiti variant of the word jipi that has mostly overtaken it in use.
jupi.definition: yippee, woohoo (interjection indicating excitement, joy or happiness); to celebrate, to win; autism, autistic, ADHD, neurodivergent, spectrum, to stim
jupi.etymology: yupi: Spanish, yippee; yippee: English, yippee; .ui: Lojban, happiness

ka.definition: (reemplazo de li, marcado para el tiempo futuro)
ka.etymology: ka: toki sapin, (future tense marker); kama: toki pona, coming

ka1.commentary:
Recoined indepentently multiple times over several years."
Originally created for toki sapin, a short-lived unfinished tokiponido created in January 2021 by jan Posuke. It survived through notes in ma pona pi toki pona, where it was the first thing that came up for "ka".
Created as part of a set with "so" for the past tense, "te" for the present tense, and contractions "miso", "mite", and "mika". Only "ka" has seen significant mention outside of toki sapin.

ka2.commentary: Borrowed from tuki tiki as a replacement for the deprecated konwe.
ka2.definition: living thing, moving being, creature; life, alive
ka2.etymology: ka: tuki tiki, being, person, creature, alive; jan: toki pona, human being, person, somebody

kalamARR.definition: el sonido que hace un pirata
kalamARR.etymology: kalama: toki pona, make sound. sound, noise, voice; arr: inglés, (imitation of pirates)

kalapisituji.commentary: la ortografía de esa palabra no está fijada y cualquier "wuwojiti" no es un error. Qué viene después que kalapis… se puede cambiar y todavía queda la misma palabra (así diviértete con kalapisituwi, kalapisituje, kalapisu…, o cualquier forma que prefieres).
kalapisituji.definition: estafa, totalmente absurdo, confundido (muchas veces a propósito)

kalijopilale.definition: un miembro del orden Caryophyllales, el cual incluye a todos los cáctus, los claveles, la betarraga, la remolacha, la acelga y varias especies de plantas carnívoras

kan.definition: con (comitativo), junto a, en compañía de
kan.etymology: kanssa: finés, with; kun: esperanto, with; cum: latín, with

kana.commentary: Sirve como una variante no obsoleta y polisémica de palabras como oni, powe, o jume que tiene una connotación neutral.
kana.definition: sueño; trance, hipnosis, alucinación; ilusión, fantasía, imaginario; narrativa, historia, mito, leyenda

kankuli.definition: algo despeinado o feo que te encanta
kankuli.etymology: scrunkly: inglés, cute in a quirky, scruffy, or dishevelled way

kapa.commentary: In a poll in 2002, "kapa" was proposed as a replacement for "nena". However, "kapa" failed to get enough votes.
kapa.definition: colina, montaña, botón, bulto, chichón [forma alternativa de la palabra nena]
kapa.etymology: kop: neerlandés, head, cup; kapo: esperanto, head; caput: latín, head

kasan.definition: legume, seed, nut, bean, soy, lentils, hard shelled fruit
kasan.etymology: kacang: Malay, bean, nut, peanut, legume

ke.definition: de acuerdo, está bien, vale, okey; (señal de reconocimiento o aceptación)

kelo.commentary: Part of a system with uka to express the colors seen by someone with protanopia or deuteranopia (red-green colorblindness).
kelo.etymology: kule: toki pona, color, pigment. category, genre, flavor. colorful, diverse; telo: toki pona, liquids e.g. water, gasoline, soda, lava, soup, oil, ink

kepa.commentary: Semantically, "kepa" describes a group or the state of a group rather than the state of one thing in a group.
kepa.definition: pattern, order, sequence, series, rhythm; arrangement, organization, decluttering; weaving, knitting

kepen.commentary: Creado como una versión acortada de kepeken por jan Tomen en 2017. No vio uso significante hasta 2020.
kepen.etymology: kepeken: toki pona, to use, by means of, with

kese.etymology: קֶשֶׁת (kéšet): hebreo, named for the Jewish LGBT organization Keshet

kewe.commentary: Part of a system with sunta to express the colors seen by someone with tritanopia (blue-yellow colorblindness).
kewe.etymology: kule: toki pona, color, pigment. category, genre, flavor. colorful, diverse; sewi: toki pona, up, top, above, highest part. divine, sacred, supernatural. awesome, inspiring, excelling

ki.commentary: Provides relativisation of subjects (see WALS 122A), but not relativisation of obliques (see WALS 123A). Unlike ki, more typical relative constructions (usually two clauses linked via ni) support both.

kokoliko.definition: gallo, pollo, aves del género Gallus

kolin.etymology: ko olin: toki pona, gloopy love

kolo.etymology: guruwe: suajili, cerdo; ingulube: Zulu, cerdo; ngulu: Kongo, cerdo; guarro, gorrino: español, cerdo; gorogoro: japonés, growl; grunt: inglés, grunt

konsi.commentary: Prácticamente desconocido fuera de la comunidad coreana de toki pona. Aparentemente se usa humorísticamente.
konsi.definition: notificar, avisar, hacer saber, hacer notar

konsuno.etymology: kon + suno: toki pona, calque of English "gaslight"

kosan.definition: proteger, defender, guardar, escudar, apuntalar, fortificar desde el exterior

kosikosa.definition: más o menos, un poco similar

kulaso.etymology: green: inglés, green; laso: toki pona, blue, green

kulu.definition: (variante abreviada de la palabra kulupu); seis (particularmente en la base numérica senaria)
kulu.etymology: kulupu: toki pona, group, community, collection

kuntu.commentary: Originally an initialism of "kalama musi tan uta" ("kmtu"), meant as an equivalent of English "LOL"; compare the formations of "omen" and "peto".
kuntu.etymology: kulupu musi tan uta: toki pona, 'fun sound from the mouth', to sing with your voice

kutopoma.commentary: Prácticamente desconocido fuera de la comunidad coreana de toki pona.
kutopoma.definition: el grupo de chat en coreano "kulupu pi toki pona pi ma Anku"
kutopoma.etymology: kulupu pi toki pona pi ma Anku: toki pona, the Toki Pona community of Korea

lan.commentary: Intended as a replacement for 'jan' in mixed-headnoun groups, or when the headnoun of the person being referred to is unknown.
lan.definition: un ser, individuo, algo que existe, algo que pueda actuar
lan.etymology: lon: toki pona, presente, existente, real, verdad. (preposición) ubicada en, durante, en, en el contexto de; wan: toki pona, único, unido. uno. unir; jan: toki pona, ser humano, persona, alguien

lijokuku.commentary: Prácticamente desconocido fuera de la comunidad coreana de toki pona.

likujo.definition: colección, surtido, menagerie, arreglo, puñado, manojo, cosecha; siete
likujo.etymology: recueil: francés acadiano, collection, ensemble, anthology

lipasa.commentary: "Coined" by kala Asi for a small-scale survey to see if people would select it.
lipasa.definition: [palabra falsa que no existe, para encontrar encuestas llenadas deshonestamente]
lipasa.etymology: липа (lipa): ruso, linden tree. (colloquial) forgery, fake

lisa.commentary: Coined in response to -isa animal words.

lo.commentary: Previously known as lu. Most of those interested in the word have agreed to change it to lo, to avoid interfering with the reserved word. Can also be combined with ja to mark modified or compound prepositions, with ja marking the end of a preposition marked by lo and the start of the prepositional object.
lo.etymology: alu: toki pona, (indicate context for a prior sentence)

loka.commentary: Coined in response to jan Sonja's survey, as a portmanteau, presumably as a joke.
loka.etymology: luka: toki pona, hand, arm, grasping appendage, tactile appendage; noka: toki pona, foot, leg, roots, locomotive appendage

lokon.definition: {un compromiso entre las palabras lukin y oko}
lokon.etymology: lukin: toki pona, look, see, eye. (preverb) try to; oko: toki pona, look, see, eye. (preverb) try to

lonsi.commentary: Used commonly in ma pona pi toki pona for much of late 2023; no set meaning other than the phrase "lonsi li lonsi".
lonsi.etymology: lon: toki pona, presente, existente, real, verdad. (preposición) ubicada en, durante, en, en el contexto de; tonsi: toki pona, nonbinary, gender nonconforming, genderqueer, transgender

lu.definition: (palabra reservada por Sonja Lang para su uso en el futuro)

lu1.etymology: alu: toki pona, (indicate context for a prior sentence)

masenta.etymology: magenta: inglés, purplish-red color; magenta: francés, purplish-red color

me.etymology: anu seme: toki pona, tag question

molusa.definition: molusco; un animal blando, por ejemplo un caracol, una babosa o una esponja
molusa.etymology: mollusk: inglés, Invertebrae related to snails, slugs, mussels, octopi (scientific nomenclature); mollusca: latín, thin shelled

natu.definition: relación, correlación, similitud; cruz, nudo, cruce, intersección; superponerse, solaparse, doblar, plegar; traspasar, sobrepasar, excederse
natu.etymology: ngã tư: vietnamita, crossroads, four-way intersection

nele.commentary: Meant to be antonymous to the 'cover, layer of privacy' meanings of 'len'. It can be applied both literally and metaphorically.
nele.definition: objeto/material transparente, falta de privacidad; traslúcido, transparente, sin obstrucciones; aclarar, hacer transparente, quitar obstrucciones
nele.etymology: len: toki pona, cloth, fabric, clothing. secret, hidden, covered

nowi.commentary: Intended as an alt to esun which implies that non-mutually-beneficial relationships are not real relationships.
nowi.definition: conectado, relacionado, unido; complementario, mutuo; intercambio
nowi.etymology: nối: vietnamita, to join, to add, to unite, to connect. to continue, to carry on, to perpetuate

nu.definition: (palabra reservada por Sonja Lang para su uso en el futuro)

nusun.commentary: Coined by jan Sonja after she was asked for a word beginning with "nu", for use in sitelen pona cartouches using nasin sitelen kalama.
nusun.etymology: nujum: Malay, astrology, fortune-telling, predicting the future. fortune-teller, oracle. star

okepuma.definition: una persona nacida durante el boom de natalidad posterior a la segunda guerra mundial, boomer, anciano grosero; (interjección/insulto)

oki.commentary: Though it may have many possible origins, it was first coined in a small group chat. It is mostly used as a variant of the toki pona words "oke" and "ke"
oki.definition: de acuerdo, está bien, vale, okey; (señal de reconocimiento o aceptación)
oki.etymology: okie: inglés, okay; okay: inglés, okay

olala.commentary: this is a hard word to explain and you've got to use it right. It's pretty much linked to anything you could use "oh là  là" or "ouh là là" for. But because it's now a full-fledged toki pona content word, it got even more powerful and versatile. It can be used for joy, surprise, excitement, admiration, compassion, empathy, sadness, disappointment, and even to show you’re interested in someone romantically. However, it's equally important (or more) to know when to *not* use the word, you wouldn’t want to commit a faux-pas!

omekalike.definition: (nombre propio) una canción de jan Usawi, (literalmente) "o moku e kala ike" (come un pescado malo)
omekalike.etymology: o moku e kala ike: toki pona, 'eat a bad fish', wishing somebody harm instead of well; o moku e kala pona: toki pona, 'eat a good fish', wishing somebody well

omen.commentary: Originally an initialism of "o moli e mi" ("omem"); compare the formations of "peto" and "kuntu".
omen.etymology: o moli e mi: toki pona, 'kill me', express exasperation or frustration sarcastically

oni.commentary:
This word is deprecated by the creator; it is considered part of the "toki oni" tokiponido instead.
The pronunciation of the second syllable was due to personal preferences of the creator, not modern Greek.
oni.definition: sueño, fantasía, juego imaginativo, visión, estado místico; hacer entrar en un trance

pa.definition: (interjección usada para expresar incredulidad, exasperación o emoción); hermano, mano, vato, tronco, weón, bro

pakola.etymology: pakala: toki pona, broken, damaged, harmed, destroyed

panke.definition: baguette; algo de cultura francesa; hacer calco del idioma francés cuando estás hablando toki pona

papa1.definition: objeto de iridio, girando y acelerando exponencialmente

papa2.commentary: Varias personas (¿diferentes?) en 2019-2021 extrañamente pusieron "papa" en sus publicaciones de Reddit / Facebook, aparentemente para promocionarla como una palabra a otras personas. Causó una mala reacción, con otras personas criticando la creación y creando otros significados para "papa".

pasila.definition: bueno, fácil; sencillo, simple, relajado, tranquilo, libre de complicaciones
pasila.etymology: facile: francés acadiano, easy; facila: esperanto, easy

pata.etymology: брат (brat): serbocroata, brother; brata: tok pisin, sibling or cousin of the same gender; brother: inglés, brother

pela.commentary: Referencia a la entrevista de Sacha Baron Cohen con Noah Chomsky.

peta.definition: verde, verdoso; vivo; ecológico, sustentable, sostenible, amistoso con el medioambiente

peto.commentary: Originally an initialism of "pana e telo oko"; compare the formations of "omen" and "kuntu".
peto.etymology: pana e telo oko: toki pona, (incomplete sentence) 'give eye-water', to cry

pipo.definition: molestar, fastidiar, incordiar; molesto, fastidioso; aburrido
pipo.etymology: pipi: toki pona, bug, insect, arachnid

pipolo.definition: algo que debería ser "kikolo" pero no es
pipolo.etymology: kikolo: toki pona; pipo: toki pona

pokasi.definition: poggies, pog, poggers (interjection indicating excitement); epic, cool, amazing, exciting

polinpin.etymology: bowling pin: inglés, bolo, pino de boliche

pomotolo.commentary: From the time management method developed by Francesco Cirillo in Berlin in the 1980s, for which people often used tomato-shaped timers. In ku, "pomotolo" was given as one of the translations of "tomato", presumably due to speakers misusing or misunderstanding the word.
pomotolo.definition: eficaz, eficiente, efectivo, útil, que da buenos resultados

poni.commentary: Occasionally comes up as a jokish corruption of "pona" (especially in a phrase "toki poni").

positu.definition: la vida, el universo, y todo lo demás

pulaso.definition: azul, ultramar, índigo, azul marino, azul cobalto
pulaso.etymology: blue: inglés, blue; laso: toki pona, blue, green

salu.commentary: 💭 Intended as an experiment in its current form, and is not meant for widespread use at this time. Meant to be used alongside a nasin that does not use either ike or pona to express value judgements (especially morality). These words are often used in a way that ignores the reasoning behind the judgement. The goal of this experimental nasin is to promote mindfulness about value itself, translating mere reaction into introspection and explanation. ⚠️ Because of the stated attachment to this particular nasin pi toki pona, it should be noted that using salu and pona/ike as good/bad at the same time goes against the point of the word's creation, and that salu is redundant in this circumstance. If you are using one, you should not use the other.
salu.definition: comfort, security, safety, stability; attention, care, caution

samu.commentary: Creado en referencia a jan Samu, un usuario de Facebook que intentó proponer una nueva palabra.
samu.etymology: jan Samu: toki pona, jan Samu

sikako.commentary: sikeko is a common misspelling from tokiponists who believe "sikako" derives from "sike ko".

sikomo.commentary: Coined in reference to the Travis Scott song ‘Sicko Mode’.
sikomo.definition: en un nivel/plano superior, iluminado, épico, brígido; en una gran medida

silapa.commentary:
Normalmente escrito en varias líneas con un "cuello" largo usando la letra "i" (originalmente la primera "a"), como así:
si
 i
 ilapa

sipi.commentary: Proposed on a forum and quickly forgotten. Notable only for being an early non pu word proposal.
sipi.etymology: sibi (dative reflexive pronoun): latín, sibi (dative reflexive pronoun)

sipije.etymology: TBH creature: inglés; TBH: inglés, to be honest

siwala.definition: the quality of being outside of understanding but accepted as reality; the quality of being not wished to be understood by one for the benefits associated with not understanding
siwala.etymology: tiwalà: tagalo, trust. faith (in someone or something). reliance, confidence, belief

slape.etymology: lape: toki pona, sleep, rest; sleep: inglés, sleep

snoweli.commentary: Originally a sitelen pona glyph with no definition, created for fun and submitted to linja lipamanka. It grew popular after its inclusion in the font "sitelen seli kiwen", after which it has come to have a definition and seen some use.
snoweli.definition: soweli with relation to the snow or cold, soweli living in predominantly cold or snowy environments (often light-colored); arctic fox
snoweli.etymology: snowman: English, snowman; soweli: toki pona, fuzzy creature, land animal, beast

sole.commentary: May refer to a state prior to a kama.
sole.definition: constante, sin cambios, inalterado, inafectado; instinto, hábito; soler
sole.etymology: soler: español, to use to, to tend to

su1.definition: [marcador de preguntas sí o no, sustituyendo el rol de li]

suke.commentary: The typo was made by jan Sonja while writing ku. The absolute majority of the community does not treat it as a newly coined word.
suke.etymology: typo of sike: toki pona, circle, sphere, cycle

sulaso.definition: turquesa, cian, celeste, azul claro, cerúleo
sulaso.etymology: cyan: inglés, the color between blue and green on the visible spectrum of light; laso: toki pona, blue, green

sunta.commentary: Part of a system with kewe to express the colors seen by someone with tritanopia (blue-yellow colorblindness).
sunta.etymology: suno: toki pona, light, shine, glow, radiance. sun, light source. brightness; uta: toki pona, mouth, lips, throat, consuming orifice

suwili.etymology: soweli: toki pona; suwi: toki pona

ta.commentary: Follows a semantically consistent pattern of "SUBJ li PV ta PRED" = "SUBJ li PV e [SUBJ li PRED]" or "[SUBJ li PRED] li PV". The top and bottom lines of the sitelen pona may be extended to the left around the pre-predicate word(s).

tankala.commentary: One-off coinage by Sonja Lang after asking about neologisms for directions.

tasun.commentary: Coined as a challenge to font creators. The sitelen pona glyph can be extended horizontally. This is usually represented in Latin characters by additional a's, e.g. "taaasun".

ten.etymology: tenpo: toki pona, time, event, situation

tenkala.commentary: Variant of tankala, suggested at the same time as an alternate tokiponization of the etymon.

tokana.definition: concentración, atención, enfoque, foco, resolución, propósito, precisión, finura; concentrarse, poner atención

toma.commentary: The typo was made by jan Sonja while writing ku. The absolute majority of the community does not treat it as a newly coined word.
toma.etymology: typo of tomo: toki pona, home, room, house, building

tona.commentary: This word was only coined to make the spoonerism "poki tona" have a meaning.

towoki.etymology: toki: toki pona, hola; OwO: emoticón, cara sorprendida como un gato

u.definition: (palabra reservada por Sonja Lang para su uso en el futuro)

uka.commentary: Part of a system with kelo to express the colors seen by someone with protanopia or deuteranopia (red-green colorblindness).
uka.etymology: uta: toki pona, mouth, lips, throat, consuming orifice; kasi: toki pona, plant, vegetation. herb, leaf

umesu.commentary: Created for the game Acrophobia (musi Ako) in response to the fact that there are few words starting with 'u' in Toki Pona.
umesu.definition: impresionar a la gente al terminar en primer lugar en un juego relacionado a Toki Pona
umesu.etymology: Umeyz: Iqglic, to amaze (someone), surprise, impress

waken.definition: wug (criatura imaginaria de un experimento lingüístico)
waken.etymology: wuggen: inglés, non-standard plural of "wug"; wug: inglés, imaginary creature resembling a bird

waleja.commentary: May be used as a preposition, e.g. "lipu waleja toki pona = a book about toki pona". However, this usage is very rare.
waleja.definition: contexto, tema, relevancia, prominencia, pertinente, de actualidad; concernir, ser relevante, ser pertinente
waleja.etymology: valdea: quenya, of moment, important; valda: quenya, worth, worthy, dear

wawajete.commentary: Creado en referencia a "wuwojiti", una secuencia de todas las sílabas CV fonotácticamente prohibidas en toki pona.
wawajete.definition: algo que parece romper las reglas pero no lo hace; rebelarse de mentira, provocación
wawajete.etymology: wuwojiti: toki pona, (all the phonotactically unviable CV syllables in Toki Pona)

we1.definition: (partícula que actúa como una transición de una oración completa a otra)
we1.etymology: güey: español, man; man: inglés, man (sentence tag)

wiwi.definition: "sí por supuesto!", "claro que sí!"; acuerdo, consentimiento; aprobar

yutu.commentary: The "y" was a typo and was meant to be "j", but got kept in anyway; compare "yupekosi".
yutu.definition: el emoji estrella que se usa en el servidor de Discord "ma pona pi toki pona" para el canal de mensajes estrellados
